**Final Project Report**

Date: Jun 25 2026		Project Name: **Milly**		Team:  Mikayil Guliyev  
Ali Safarli  
Ilkin Ismayilov  
Yusif Xankishiyev  
Omar Ismayilov

---

**Project Summary**

**The idea**

* What is your product, and what problem does it solve?

Milly improves the experience for both restaurant customers and staff. Diners scan a table QR code to browse the menu, place orders, and pay from their phone. Staff approve orders and manage the menu, tables, and bills in one real-time dashboard — reducing wait times and manual order-taking.

* Who is it for?

Restaurants and their staff (managers and waiters who handle orders, menus, tables, and QR codes), and dining customers who want to order and pay at the table without waiting for a waiter.

**Starting point**

* Are you continuing your first project, or starting a new one? Check the two options in the technical requirements.  
* If you continue, what is the current status, and what will you add, improve, or fix?

We are starting a new project. Milly is a separate full-stack application. Technical details are listed below.

**The backend**

* What are your two main resources with full CRUD? For example, products and orders.  
1. Menu Items — staff create, read, update, and remove items from the restaurant catalog.  
2. Orders — create (customer places order), read (staff board and customer bill), update (approve, reject, add items to approved order, close when paid). 

We also manage Tables (create, read, deactivate) as a supporting resource for QR codes. Tables are not counted as one of the two main CRUD resources above, but Managers have full table management in the staff portal.

* What roles will exist, and what can each role do? You need at least two.

System role: USER — every signed-up account. ADMIN system role is not publicly accessible.   
Venue roles (per restaurant): Manager — menu, tables, QR, invitations, orders, payments; Waiter — orders only (view, approve, reject, close). Customers have no account.

* Which pages or actions need a logged in user?

/login, /register-venue, /join-venue, and /venue/{venueId}/staff require a global session. Venue role is checked on every staff API call — no membership or wrong role returns 403\. Manager-only: menu, tables, QR, invitations. /table/\[id\] stays public.

**The frontend**

* How many pages will the frontend have? List them. You need at least three.

/ — home (Join Venue, Register Venue).   
/login — sign in / sign up.   
/register-venue — create a venue.   
/join-venue — my venues \+ join via invitation.   
/venue/\[id\]/staff — staff dashboard.   
/table/\[tableId\] — customer QR flow.

![][image1]

* Which of those pages are protected and need login?

/login is public.  
/register-venue, /join-venue, and /venue/\[id\]/staff require login.   
/ and /table/\[id\] are public. UI hides actions by venue role; backend enforces regardless.

* Where does a logged out user get redirected?

User on log out is redirected to home page /.  
Tapping Join or Register on / goes to /login; after auth, user lands on /join-venue or /register-venue. Opening a protected route without a session redirects to /login.

**Connecting the two parts**

* How does the frontend talk to the backend?

REST for all reads and writes. STOMP/WebSocket for push after mutations — clients subscribe and refresh via REST. Staff topics are venue-scoped: /topic/venue/{id}/staff. Customers use anonymous WS on /topic/table/{id}.  
![][image2]

* Where is the login token stored, and how is it used on later requests?

HttpOnly cookies (access \+ refresh). Browser sends cookies on REST calls. WebSocket uses POST /api/v1/ws-ticket to get a 30s single-use ticket — JWT never goes in the WS URL. Expired access token triggers refresh; 401 on refresh means logged out.

* What happens on logout?

Backend invalidates session and clears cookies. Frontend closes WebSocket and redirects to the home page.

**The plan of work**

* A rough timeline from now until the deadline.

Week 1: project setup, global auth (JWT \+ OAuth), venue module (create, join, invitations), menu \+ orders CRUD, tables, REST integration, WebSocket tickets \+ venue-scoped topics, deploy Render/Vercel, billing/payments, role-based UI.

Week 2: invitation management, testing, presentation.

* The first few tasks you will put on your Trello or Jira board.

First tasks to put into Jira board will cover project core implementation, such as defining architectural boundaries, setting entities and domain models,   
implementation of main frontend pages \+ use cases on backend. 

Once the core layer is ready, contract related tasks will be added, to connect frontend and backend.

First few ticket summaries:  
\[DOCS\] \- Complete Final Project report   
\[DESIGN\] \- Provide High level System Design   
\[DESIGN\] \- Provide WebSocket Flow Documentation   
\[DESIGN\] Auth & venue security flow  
\[DEV\] Backend — scaffold Clean Architecture modules  
\[DEV\] Frontend — scaffold module structure & routes
